title: Keep the main process responsive
description: Never run heavy or blocking work on the main process, since it owns every window and the app's event loop.
body: Use when doing CPU-heavy or synchronous work (parsing, crypto, large file I/O). Do not run synchronous/blocking calls on the main process, and do not assume one window's work is isolated. Actions: move heavy work to a utility process, worker thread, or the renderer, use async APIs, and keep the main process orchestrating only. Validation signals include all windows staying interactive during heavy operations and no main-process event-loop stalls in a profile. Risk boundary: a blocked main process freezes every window simultaneously (the whole app hangs), unlike a single renderer stall — main-process blocking is an app-wide reliability defect.
